steps.title: Find the axis (pencil)
steps.description: Make sure you're in pencil mode. Lightly sketch a long diagonal line for the stem — the sunflower is lying on its side, so the axis runs across the canvas, not up and down.

steps.title: Block the head (pencil)
steps.description: Still in pencil, sketch a circle at one end of the stem for the flower head. Inside it, mark a smaller circle for the dark center where the seeds sit.

steps.title: Plan the petals (pencil)
steps.description: Around the outer circle, lightly mark short radiating strokes to show where each petal will point. Space them unevenly — natural is better than perfectly symmetrical.

steps.title: Switch to ink and trace the petals
steps.description: Switch to ink mode. Working around the head, draw each petal as two confident curves meeting at a point. Don't worry about counting — let some petals overlap and some be partly hidden behind others.

steps.title: Ink the seed center (ink)
steps.description: Trace the inner circle, then fill it lightly with a scattered grid of small dots or tiny crosshatched marks. The texture suggests seeds without drawing every one.

steps.title: Ink the stem and a leaf (ink)
steps.description: Trace the stem as two roughly parallel lines along your axis. Branch off one leaf partway along — two curves meeting at a tapered point, with a short vein down the middle.

steps.title: Clean up
steps.description: Switch to the eraser and remove any pencil guide lines that distract from the ink drawing.
